analysis.mistake: 1. Logo size classes used ** (all descendants) instead of * (direct children), unintentionally targeting SVG internals. 2. Fixed state activated when element reached viewport top instead of when margin area reached top.
analysis.expected: 1. Logo sizing should use * selector to target only SVG wrapper, not internal SVG elements. 2. Fixed mode should activate when margin area (not the element itself) reaches viewport top.
analysis.rootCause: 1. Developer Agent applied standard descendant-selector pattern without accounting for SVG internal structure. 2. Agent calculated fixed-state threshold based on element position alone, not accounting for header's margin-top spacing above the element.

suggestions.content: **Logo sizing in headers:** Use direct child selector `*:` (not `**:`) for sizing classes on SVG wrappers to avoid targeting internal SVG elements (paths, groups, etc.). Descendant selectors will apply size constraints to nested SVG internals, breaking icon rendering.
suggestions.rationale: Prevents accidental styling of SVG internal structure when applying sizing to logo containers.

suggestions.content: **Fixed positioning calculation in Header block:** When implementing sticky/fixed header behavior, account for the header's margin-top in the fixed-state threshold calculation. Subtract marginTop from the element's scrollPosition to determine when the margin area (not the element itself) reaches the viewport top. Fixed mode should activate earlier to preserve margin spacing.
suggestions.rationale: Ensures fixed positioning activates at the correct scroll position, accounting for header margin as part of the active region rather than trigger point.
